steps.title: Identify the angle configuration
steps.content: Three lines intersect at point P: a horizontal line and two diagonal lines. Looking at the upper side of the horizontal line, the angles $x°$, an angle formed between the two diagonals, and $z°$ span from the left horizontal ray to the right horizontal ray. The angle between the two diagonals is labeled $y°$ in the figure.

steps.title: Apply the straight angle property
steps.content:
The three angles on the upper side of the horizontal line must sum to a straight angle. Therefore:
$$x° + y° + z° = 180°$$
Substituting $x = 65$ and $y = 75$:
$$65 + 75 + z° = 180°$$
$$140 + z° = 180°$$
$$z° = 40°$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
